params.name: Wegener's Map
params.description:
On Jul 4, 1910 AD, Wegener discovered the coincidence of the contour lines of various continents from a map of the world, and thus developed the theory of continental drift.
The label-filled map belonged to German geologist Wegener. One early morning in his hospital bed, he found a world map hanging on the wall. Inadvertently, he realized that the contours of the two sides of the ocean had extraordinary correspondence. Based on this discovery, he researched for several years before proposing …
